Description: The exact date the shipment was picked up from the origin facility.
The ISO country code of the origin warehouse (e.g., 'US', 'GB').
The ISO country code of the destination.
The destination postal code / zip code.
The standard, static transit time quoted to the customer by the legacy matrix.
The true number of days it took for the shipment to arrive. Used as the primary target variable for the regression models.
Binary flag (1 = On-Time, 0 = Late/Delayed). Used to calculate historical Net Service Level (NSL).
The day of the week the shipment was picked up (0 = Monday, 6 = Sunday).
The month of pickup (1 = Jan, 12 = Dec), capturing seasonal volume spikes.
Binary flag (1 = Yes, 0 = No) indicating if the package was picked up on a Saturday or Sunday.
The rolling historical average of actual transit days for a specific Origin -> Destination country pair, strictly using past data.
A rolling 7-day metric capturing the percentage of delayed shipments at a specific destination postal code. Serves as a real-time network congestion indicator.
Total historical shipments to a specific postal code.
The percentage of shipments to this postal code that missed their SLA.
The standard deviation of transit time to this postal code (variance/volatility).
The K-Means assigned behavioral risk profile (0-3), mapping to actionable groups like 'Golden Lanes' or 'Chronic Bottlenecks'.
LightGBM prediction for the realistic median transit time.
LightGBM prediction for the conservative, 90th percentile transit time.
The baseline Random Forest median prediction.
The P50 prediction adjusted via Isolation Forest. (+4 days added if flagged as an anomaly, +1 day otherwise).
Weibull AFT prediction: the day the package hits a 50% probability of delivery.
Weibull AFT prediction: the day the package hits a 90% probability of delivery.
The final transit quote assigned by the Phase 4 Business Logic, which evaluates the variance between P50 and P90 before deciding which to use.
Simulated binary flag (1 = On-Time, 0 = Late) used to calculate the AI's hypothetical Net Service Level (NSL) improvement.
